best.name: ip-api.com
best.maintained: active (commercial service, free non-commercial tier; 45 req/min over HTTP)
best.notes: Wired in src/tools/ip_tools.py (IpGeoTool). City/region/country/lat-lon + ISP/org/ASN. Free tier is HTTP-only and 45 req/min; do not use over HTTPS without a key.

best.name: IPinfo.io
best.notes: Wired in src/tools/ip_tools.py (IpInfoTool). Geolocation + ASN/org + hostname. Best second geo source for cross-checking ip-api.

runner_up.name: RIPEstat maxmind-geo-lite / geoloc
runner_up.notes: Authoritative-registry-adjacent geo from RIPE NCC, no key. Coarser than ip-api/ipinfo but useful as a neutral third source. Same RIPEstat base used for ASN/BGP below.

capabilities.gaps: All free IP-geo sources are city-level estimates; none give rooftop accuracy. No free reverse-geo to physical address (paid only). Two-source agreement (ip-api + ipinfo) is the practical confidence ceiling.

best.name: AbuseIPDB Check
best.notes: Free tier = 1000 checks/day (3000 if domain-verified). Returns abuseConfidenceScore (0-100), totalReports, lastReportedAt, usageType, isp, domain, isTor, countryCode. The single best free crowd-sourced abuse-reputation source; trivial to wire. Requires free account key.

best.name: ThreatFox (abuse.ch) IOC search
best.maintained: active (abuse.ch; now requires free Auth-Key header as of 2024 policy change)
best.notes: Wired in src/tools/social_tools.py (ThreatFoxTool). Confirms whether IP is a known malware C2/IOC with malware family + threat_type. NOTE: abuse.ch now requires a free Auth-Key header; the wired tool may need a key added to keep working.

runner_up.name: AlienVault OTX IP reputation/pulses
runner_up.notes: Free key. Pulse memberships, passive DNS, malware. Good breadth complement to AbuseIPDB's crowd reports; noisier/less normalized scoring.

capabilities.gaps: No single free source gives a clean malicious/benign verdict; AbuseIPDB is crowd-confidence, ThreatFox/OTX are IOC-membership. VirusTotal IP report (free key, 4 req/min) would add multi-engine consensus but is rate-limited; not wired.

best.name: GreyNoise Community API
best.notes: THE tool for this capability; nothing else in the stack classifies scanner vs benign noise. Returns noise(bool), riot(bool), classification(benign|malicious|unknown), name(org), last_seen, link. Unauthenticated = ~10 lookups/day; free account key = 50 lookups/week. Low quota -> wire as an on-demand single-IP enrichment, not a bulk pass. IPv4 only.

runner_up.name: Shodan InternetDB (tags field)
runner_up.notes: Already implemented (see open_ports below). Its tags[] field flags 'compromised','self-signed','scanner' etc. Not a true noise classifier but the only no-quota free signal of mass-scanner/honeypot character.

capabilities.gaps: GreyNoise Community quota is very low (10/day anon). No free unlimited scanner-noise classifier exists. Censys/Shodan paid tiers and GreyNoise RIOT (paid) are the only high-volume options. IPv6 unsupported by GreyNoise Community.

best.name: Shodan InternetDB
best.maintained: active (free Shodan service, no key, no published rate cap)
best.notes: Wired in src/tools/ip_tools.py (ShodanInternetDBTool). Returns ports[], cpes[], hostnames[], tags[], vulns[] (CVEs). Best free no-key port/service source. IPv4 only. No raw banners (use full Shodan API w/ key for banners).

best.name: Netlas.io Host Info
best.maintained: active (2026; free tier ~50 req/day on registered key)
best.notes: Best Shodan/Censys complement with a usable free tier. Host Info aggregates scan responses (ports, banners, protocols), certs, DNS, whois in one call. Adds real banners that InternetDB lacks. Python SDK + CLI available.

runner_up.name: Censys Platform API (host)
runner_up.notes: Deepest banner/service data but the 2026 Platform migration moved to a credit-based Personal Access Token model with no clear free automation tier; API calls consume credits. Keep as paid runner-up; prefer Netlas for free banner depth.

capabilities.gaps: Free no-key option (InternetDB) gives ports+CVEs but no banners and IPv4-only. Banners require a key (Netlas free tier, or paid Shodan/Censys). No free IPv6 port-scan data source.

best.name: RIPEstat Data API
best.notes: Best free no-key ASN/BGP/netblock source; authoritative RIR data. as-overview -> holder name + announce status; announced-prefixes -> all netblocks for an ASN; network-info -> containing prefix + announcing ASN for an IP (IPv4 AND IPv6); prefix-overview -> who announces a prefix. No request limit (register if >1000/day), 8 concurrent/IP.

best.name: BGPView API
best.run: GET https://api.bgpview.io/asn/{selector}  ;  GET https://api.bgpview.io/asn/{selector}/prefixes  ;  GET https://api.bgpview.io/ip/{ip}  ;  GET https://api.bgpview.io/asn/{selector}/peers
best.notes: Best complement to RIPEstat: cleaner JSON for ASN metadata (name, description, country, RIR, website, abuse email), peers/upstreams/downstreams, and prefix lists. /ip/{ip} maps IP->ASN+prefix. No key, no published cap. Excellent for ASN-relationship pivoting RIPEstat does less cleanly.

runner_up.name: IPinfo (ASN extraction)
runner_up.notes: Already implemented; extracts ASN+org from an IP. Convenient quick IP->ASN but only one prefix/holder, no BGP relationships. Use RIPEstat/BGPView for full netblock/peer data.

capabilities.gaps: RIPEstat + BGPView cover registry/BGP fully and free. Historical BGP routing changes / RPKI validation depth and IRR object detail are thinner; RIPEstat has RPKI-validation and whois endpoints if needed, otherwise paid (e.g. bgp.tools, RADb) for deep historical.

excluded.reason: not an IP tool; free tier folded into Sublime Security (flagged in audit).
OSS abandoned after Intel 471 acquisition (2022), 2+ yrs stale; not wiring as orchestration.
Legacy Search API deprecated in 2026 Platform migration; new Platform API is credit-based with no clear free automation tier -> kept as paid runner-up under open_ports, not a free pick.
Full Shodan host API (banners/full data) requires a paid membership key; free InternetDB covers the no-key port/CVE need. Add later only if a key is provisioned.
Fraud/proxy/VPN IP scoring is good but free tier is small and the value (proxy/VPN detection) overlaps GreyNoise+AbuseIPDB; defer unless proxy-detection becomes a requirement.
bgp.he.net (Hurricane Electric) is a manual web UI with no documented JSON API; covered by BGPView/RIPEstat programmatically.
